bool write_data_with_check(std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer, uint32_t header, const std::vector<uint32_t>& items) {
    // Create a buffer_adaptor for the buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> adaptor(buffer);

    // Calculate the required space
    const size_t required_space = sizeof(header) + (items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t));

    // Check if the buffer has enough space

    }

    // Write the header at the write pointer

    // Now, create a binary_stream that uses the buffer (not the adaptor) and write the items
    hexi::binary_stream<std::vector<uint8_t>> stream(buffer);
    stream.write_container(items);

    return true;
}

```cpp
PacketHeader read_header_with_span(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Remove const from the buffer
    auto non_const_buffer = const_cast<std::span<uint8_t>>(buffer);

    // Create a buffer_adaptor from the non-const buffer
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>> adaptor(non_const_buffer);

    // Create a binary_stream from the buffer_adaptor
    hexi::binary_stream<hexi::buffer_adaptor<std::span<uint8_t>>> stream(adaptor);

    // Use the binary_stream's view() method to get a span of the header data (with terminator 0)
    auto header_span = stream.view(0);

    // Check if the returned span has the correct size (sizeof(PacketHeader))
    if (header_span.size() != sizeof(PacketHeader)) {
        return PacketHeader{};
    }

    // Copy the data from the span to the PacketHeader
    PacketHeader header;
    std::memcpy(&header, header_span.data(), sizeof(header));

    return header;
}
```
